- Bottom: (43, 13), (43, 19), (43, 25), (43, 31) - 4 blocks (but wait, I clicked on bottom 4, so they should be cleared)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']

print(f"Green: {len(green)}")
for g in green:
    b = g['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in b)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in b)
    print(f"  ({min_r},{min_c})")

if player:
    pb = player[0]['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in pb)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in pb)
    print(f"Player: ({min_r},{min_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

    actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 43, 'col': c})

# Move RIGHT

# Top 2 (bottom to top)
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 7, 'col': 31})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 1, 'col': 31})

action(actions)

# Check state

nodes = seg['nodes']
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
result = last_action_result
print(f"Green: {len(green)}, game_over={result.get('game_over', False)}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed', False)}")
for g in green:
    b = g['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in b)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in b)
    print(f"  ({min_r},{min_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
